WamaiDoingThis's seasonal campaign represents a highly structured effort in competitive ranking. Across a deep set of matches, they have operated as a critical force in the competitive hierarchy of their team.

WamaiDoingThis (yeetingyeti) represents the critical structural backbone of the stack. While posting a lower individual K/D of 0.61, their massive utility presence with Mute (637 rounds, 60.6% Win Rate) and Aruni (249 rounds, 60.2% Win Rate) actively wins rounds in the background. In Ranked 2.0, support play must be highly disciplined: avoiding early visual gunfights and maximizing gadget uptime.

Season Objective

Increase individual survival rate to 55%+ and achieve Gold 2 by leveraging Mute/Nomad utility setups.

Pro-League Analytical Verdict

The seasonal win rate of 44.7% represents an exact delta of -5.0% compared to lifetime performance, confirming that individual combat mechanics (K/D: 0.48) must be integrated into team support systems. In Ranked 2.0, round conversion is the ultimate metric of success.

Map Winrate Overview

Attack vs Defence Win%

Map Deep Dive

Comfort Zones (Top 3 Comfort Maps)

Kafe Dostoyevsky (31 matches | 64.5% Win% | 0.51 K/D): Defensive anchoring is dialled in at 65.2%, providing a reliable foundation that absorbs lost attack rounds. K/D of 0.51 tracks with your overall map average, so wins are coming from positioning, not just raw frags. 31 matches gives a solid read on this map.
Bank (24 matches | 62.5% Win% | 0.44 K/D): Defensive anchoring is dialled in at 60.2%, providing a reliable foundation that absorbs lost attack rounds. Interestingly, K/D (0.44) is slightly below your 0.50 average — wins come from smart site holds rather than kill volume. Only 24 matches — promising numbers, but the sample is still building.
Lair (9 matches | 55.6% Win% | 0.43 K/D): Defence is the engine here — 63.6% def WR carries the overall number while attack sits at just 40.6%. Interestingly, K/D (0.43) is slightly below your 0.50 average — wins come from smart site holds rather than kill volume. Only 9 matches — promising numbers, but the sample is still building.

Struggle Zones (Bottom 3 Struggle Maps)

Clubhouse (34 matches | 32.4% Win% | 0.40 K/D): Both sides are bleeding: 32.4% on attack and 46.8% on defence. This isn't a one-side fix — site selection and anchor positioning are both off. K/D drops to 0.40 here, so the map layout itself is putting you in unfavourable fights. ESR of 0.43 confirms early-round struggles — opening engagements are going south too often.
Theme Park (6 matches | 16.7% Win% | 0.47 K/D): Both sides are bleeding: 33.3% on attack and 44.4% on defence. This isn't a one-side fix — site selection and anchor positioning are both off. K/D drops to 0.47 here, so the map layout itself is putting you in unfavourable fights. ESR of 0.33 confirms early-round struggles — opening engagements are going south too often.
Emerald Plains (2 matches | 0.0% Win% | 0.60 K/D): Only 2 matches played — this could be noise rather than a real weakness. The 0% win rate is alarming on paper but statistically meaningless at this sample size.

Attack vs Defence Asymmetry Rules

Implement these map-specific tactical corrections immediately to bridge the offense-defense discrepancy:

Chalet (31.3% Att vs 62.9% Def WR — 31.6% Gap): Garage-focused attacks are getting read. Mix in kitchen/trophy executes to keep defenders from stacking utility on one side. Drone the wine cellar rotation before committing bodies.
Skyscraper (32.5% Att vs 63.6% Def WR — 31.1% Gap): The 32.5% attack win rate means entries through tea room and geisha are being shut down hard. Run exhibition hall wrap-arounds and force defenders to deal with cross-angles they can't hold simultaneously.
Villa (39.1% Att vs 63.2% Def WR — 24.1% Gap): The 2F study/aviator default is heavily pre-aimed by defenders. Instead of dry-peeking horizontal angles, establish vertical pressure from BELOW (1F Kitchen and Dining Room) using soft breachers to displace anchors in vault/desk, while taking 2F Astronomy control and securing the Astro hatch to block late-round roamer rotates.
Lair (40.6% Att vs 63.6% Def WR — 23.0% Gap): Attack is holding at 40.6% which is workable — minor improvements to drone economy and entry timing will push this further.
Border (38.5% Att vs 59.1% Def WR — 20.6% Gap): Armoury pushes get cut off by CCTV rotates. Maintain permanent pressure on the workshop side to prevent flanks while the entry team pushes through ventilation.

Operator Coaching Engine

Mute Defender

222 Rounds
K/D Ratio
0.55
Win Rate
56.3%

PROS: Outstanding utility denial, achieving a superb 56.3% win rate and 0.55 K/D over 222 rounds.

FOCUS AREAS: Sloppy jammer placement around double walls. Ensure your jammers are fully aligned to block both hard breaches and intelligence drones.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Oregon, block laundry stairs completely during prep phase using tight stairwell jammer setups to keep defensive layouts hidden.

Aruni Defender

188 Rounds
K/D Ratio
0.73
Win Rate
56.9%

PROS: Excellent laser gate area denial with 188 rounds played, maintaining a K/D of 0.73 and 56.9% win rate.

FOCUS AREAS: Failing to reset Surya Gates after they've been deactivated. Always shoot the emitter at the top of the doorframe once the Surya gate's cooldown finishes (green light indicator) to reactivate the laser grid.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Clubhouse, place Surya gates on the main dirt tunnel entrance to block quick basement entries and force attackers to waste utility.

Nomad Attacker

155 Rounds
K/D Ratio
0.60
Win Rate
36.8%

PROS: Superb flank security and roamer denial, securing 155 rounds with a K/D of 0.60.

FOCUS AREAS: Placing Airjabs in plain sight. Keep your gadgets tucked behind doorway moldings to prevent active defender shots.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Border, place Airjabs on ventilation runouts to completely eliminate roamer jump-outs, securing your hard breacher's angles.

Rauora Attacker

146 Rounds
K/D Ratio
0.30
Win Rate
33.6%

PROS: Incredible entry and post-plant area control with the D.O.M. Panel Launcher across 146 rounds, maintaining a 33.6% win rate and 0.30 K/D.

FOCUS AREAS: Misplaced mesh panels that block active attacker lines or fail to lock down crucial rotates. Avoid trapping teammates in site corridors during late-round executes.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Chalet, fire your D.O.M. panels into the kitchen rotations or library double door from a safe distance. This forces defenders to expose themselves to open/close the panel or waste heavy explosives (like Impact Grenades or C4) to clear it, securing a safe flank watch during the main snowmobile garage push.

Smoke Defender

118 Rounds
K/D Ratio
0.32
Win Rate
55.1%

PROS: Premium area denial and late-round stall, clocking 118 rounds with a K/D of 0.32 and 55.1% win rate.

FOCUS AREAS: Throwing canisters too early in the round. Save all three canisters for the final 45 seconds where you can completely block doorways and win by time denial.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Bank, hold server stairs and use your gas canisters to completely shut down the server corridor when the attack tries to plant in the final seconds.

PROS: Strong area-denial and post-plant lockups, posting a K/D of 0.50 over 109 rounds.

FOCUS AREAS: Executing executes before active roamer sweep. Ensure Trax Stingers are deployed on rotation stairs.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Bank, throw Stingers on square stairs and elevator shafts to completely seal basement rotates during execute phase.

FOCUS AREAS: Dying with EMP grenades in your pocket. Always stay behind your hard breacher and clear electric/mute denial BEFORE looking for gunfights.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Clubhouse, throw your EMPs onto the roof above CCTV outer wall to disable Kaid claws placed on internal ceiling beams without exposing yourself to vertical angles.

FOCUS AREAS: Over-committing to visual barricades in pre-cleared lanes. Ensure your Kiba barriers are deployed dynamically in late-round trades.

TACTICAL STRATEGY: On Skyscraper, deploy Kiba barriers on tea room rotations to establish bulletproof head-holes while protecting anchors from terrace sightlines.

Priority Improvement Matrix

Focus Area Current State Target State Strategic Rationale
1. Entry Success Rate (ESR) Optimization Average ESR: 0.37 across Ranked map pool. Achieve a target ESR of 0.45+ through active pre-drone entry. Winning the opening duel increases the team round win probability by over 30% in competitive lobbies.
2. Attack Execute Conversion Sub-optimal round wins on comfort maps due to slow executes. Secure attack win rates above 48% across all 17 competitive maps. Accelerating structural breaches prevents defenders from using late-round denial utility (e.g. Smoke/Tubarão).
3. Operator Selection Discipline Flexing to lower-value operators on key sites. Restrict operator pool to top-performing stars (Azami, Thorn, Mute, Thermite). Committing to high-success index operators maximizes background round utility and gunfight effectiveness.
4. Flank & Intel Denial Vulnerable to roamer pinches on deep executes. Deploy 100% reliable flank watch using Nomad Airjabs and Gridlock Trax. Blocking defender rotations allows the main entry stack to concentrate fully on site clears.
5. Offense-Defense Symmetry Significant win rate variance between Attack and Defense. Achieve less than 12% win rate variance between attack and defense sides. Climbs in Ranked 2.0 depend heavily on winning rounds on both sides, avoiding uncoordinated clutch scenarios.

Ban & Veto Strategy (Y11S1 — Ranked 2.0)

Top 2 Maps to Ban

Emerald Plains

0.0% WR | 0.60 K/D. Statistically the poorest map. Uncoordinated vertical sweeps lead to structural site defeats. Ban immediately in drafting phase.

Theme Park

16.7% WR | 0.47 K/D. Highly dangerous terrain where uncoordinated attacks result in early breacher deaths. Deny from the map draft pool.

Top 2 Maps to Protect

Kafe Dostoyevsky

64.5% WR | 0.51 K/D. Premier map comfort. Exceptional horizontal spacing and trade execution. Pick immediately if left open.

Bank

62.5% WR | 0.44 K/D. Flawless site anchor layouts and high defensive win rates. Protect and leverage established angles.

Trio Stack Synergy & climbing Tips

Since WamaiDoingThis acts as the team's vital anchor, his survival is key to blocking intelligence and denying plants. On defense, play close to Amlenk's anchors (such as Azami/Lesion), allowing the Champion MMR of Amlenk to trade him out if pushed. In roam phases, communicate active roamer paths to Covetous_Demon (Entry/Roamer) so Covetous can pinch them. On attack, Nomad/Gridlock flank watch is WamaiDoingThis's priority. Place Airjabs to lock down runouts, allowing Amlenk and Covetous_Demon to focus entirely on mechanical site entry.

Veto Watchlist

Bank (51.9% WR) and Border (50.0% WR) are situational comfort zones. High individual combat effectiveness is maintained here, but round wins are lost during uncoordinated entries. Only pick if your stack has dedicated support roles pre-assigned.

3 Actionable Climbing Tips

1. Hidden MMR Coordinated Queueing

Pre-stack with your trio instead of solo-queuing, which heavily penalizes individual MMR in Ranked 2.0 matchmaking systems.

2. Utility-First Attack Executes

Establish drone routes to clear defender utility before the 1:15 mark, securing safe entries.

3. Breach-Denial Tricking

Coordinate active Jäger/Wamai interceptors to protect Bandit/Kaid wall denial from vertical hatches.
